id: # ZTA L4 Microsegmentation Conformance Matrix
# Each row is one synthetic probe (`nc -zvw5 dst:port`) issued from a
# busybox pod created in `src_ns` with the supplied labels. `expected`
# is what Cilium's L4 policy engine SHOULD do:
#   ALLOW = TCP handshake completes within 5s (nc exit 0)
#   DROP  = connection times out / refused (nc exit non-zero)
# `src_labels` is a comma-separated list of `key=value` pairs added to
# the test pod via `kubectl run --labels`. Add labels here to match the
# `fromEndpoints` selector of a CNP and exercise its allow path. Leave
# blank to spawn a vanilla pod (identity = just-the-namespace)
# is the right shape for testing negative / cross-tier flows.
# `cnp_ref` links the row to the CNP YAML file + rule name that the
# matrix entry exercises
src_ns: so a failure points the operator straight at

id: # the offending policy.
# A leading `#` marks a comment row (skipped by the runner).
# Positive — flows that ARE in the allow list (expect ALLOW)
# DNS from anywhere to kube-system/kube-dns:53 — broad allow (23-kube-system.yaml).
# Tested from 5 representative tiers to make sure CoreDNS L7 ingress fix sticks.
# Kafka ingress from any pod in job7189-apps ns (selector by NS only — safe to test
# with a vanilla busybox pod
# Service name is `kafka-svc` (headless
# DNS name `kafka.data.svc.cluster.local` does NOT resolve.
# Negative — flows that should be DENIED by default-deny + no matching allow
# Cross-tier bypass: an unprivileged pod in T3 (job7189-apps) tries to reach
# MySQL directly. Only `identity-service` and `keycloak`/`vault`/`phpmyadmin`
# are whitelisted on :3306; a vanilla pod must be dropped.
# Kong admin API must never be reachable from outside the gateway namespace.
# Random pod in management can't talk to Kafka — only apps tier.
# Random pod in monitoring (NOT prometheus SA) can't scrape Kong status.

description: Random monitoring pod -> Kong :8100 (no prometheus SA)

id: # Apps -> Vault API is INTENTIONALLY allowed by `allow-vault-api-ingress`
# (11-vault.yaml): both `job7189-apps` and `data` namespaces are
# whitelisted on :8200 so workloads can pull dynamic DB credentials
# directly from the Vault Database Secrets Engine. Tightening this to
# a per-app allow list (only services that actually consume vault) is
# tracked for Phase 2D — see knowledge-base/38-microseg-conformance-test.md §7.

description: Apps -> Vault API (dynamic DB creds — intentional)

id: # Management ns is NOT in the vault allow list — proper cross-tier deny.
# Apps -> Keycloak :8080 is INTENTIONALLY allowed by `allow-keycloak-ingress`
# (12-security.yaml) so workloads in `job7189-apps` can fetch the OIDC
# discovery document (`/realms/<r>/.well-known/openid-configuration`) and
# JWKS (`/protocol/openid-connect/certs`) directly — Kong does not proxy
# these public-key endpoints. This is documented as an explicit
# microsegmentation deviation in chap-4. Tightening this to a per-app
# allow list is tracked for Phase 2D (L7 policy: restrict by URL path).
# Random pod can't reach OPA decision endpoint (only Kong is whitelisted).
# Anomaly-injection scenarios (NOT executed by --quick / --full modes —
# only the dedicated --anomaly mode runs these). Captured here so the
# Phase 2 ML detector (Tetragon kprobe + statistical UEBA) has a
# repeatable corpus of "known bad" behaviour for training / evaluation.

description: exec /bin/sh inside container that never uses a shell
Egress to threat-intel CIDR (FireHOL block list)
